# Econumo

> Econumo is an open-source budgeting app for personal, couple, and family finances — multi-currency, joint accounts, and shared budgets. Cloud or self-hosted.

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://econumo.com/docs.md)
- [Editions](https://econumo.com/docs/edition.md)

- [Classifications](https://econumo.com/docs/user-guide/classifications.md)
- [Budgets](https://econumo.com/docs/user-guide/budgets.md)
- [Shared access](https://econumo.com/docs/user-guide/shared-access.md)

- [User profile](https://econumo.com/docs/user-guide/user-profile.md)
- [CSV Import & Export](https://econumo.com/docs/user-guide/csv-import-export.md)
- [Apps](https://econumo.com/docs/apps.md)
- [Self-Hosting](https://econumo.com/docs/self-hosting.md)

- [Debug](https://econumo.com/docs/self-hosting/debug.md)
- [API](https://econumo.com/docs/api.md)
- [FAQ](https://econumo.com/docs/faq.md)

- [Principles of Finance: A Practical Guide for Family Budgeting](https://econumo.com/posts/principles-of-finance.md): Discover the principles of finance and how risk, return, and cash flow shape your family's budget and future with practical, simple steps.
- [What Does Secured Bond Mean An Investor's Simple Guide](https://econumo.com/posts/what-does-secured-bond-mean.md): Wondering what does secured bond mean? Learn how secured bonds work, what collateral means for your safety, and how they fit into a smart investment plan.
- [Can You Have an IRA and a 401k? Supercharge Your Savings](https://econumo.com/posts/can-you-have-an-ira-and-a-401-k.md): Wondering can you have an IRA and a 401k? Yes. Learn the rules, contribution limits, and strategies to maximize your retirement savings with both accounts.
- [7 Life-Changing Benefits of Paying Off Mortgage Early in 2026](https://econumo.com/posts/benefits-of-paying-off-mortgage-early.md): Unlock financial freedom with these 7 benefits of paying off mortgage early. Learn how to save thousands in interest, build wealth, and reduce stress.
- [Boost Your Score: remove collections from credit report](https://econumo.com/posts/remove-collections-from-credit-report.md): Discover proven steps to remove collections from credit report with disputes, negotiations, and validation letters to boost your score.
- [How to Improve Your Credit Score: Quick, Actionable Tips](https://econumo.com/posts/how-to-improve-your-credit-score.md): Discover how to improve your credit score with practical, actionable tips on payment history, utilization, and credit mix to boost your finances.
- [How to Pay Off Debt Faster and Find Financial Freedom](https://econumo.com/posts/how-to-pay-off-debt-faster.md): Learn how to pay off debt faster with proven strategies like the snowball and avalanche methods. Our guide offers actionable budgeting and income tips.
- [How to Save for a House Your Practical Home Buying Guide](https://econumo.com/posts/save-for-a-house.md): Ready to save for a house? Our practical guide breaks down budgeting, down payments, and smart savings strategies to help you reach your homeownership goals.
- [How Much to Save Per Paycheck (how much to save per paycheck): A Practical Guide](https://econumo.com/posts/how-much-to-save-per-paycheck.md): Curious how much to save per paycheck? Discover practical tips, real-life examples, and a simple plan to boost your savings today.
- [A Simple Guide to Income and Expenses](https://econumo.com/posts/income-and-expenses.md): Take control of your finances with our guide to tracking income and expenses. Learn simple budgeting methods to save more and reach your financial goals.
- [The 12 Best Monthly Expense Tracker Printable Resources for 2026](https://econumo.com/posts/monthly-expense-tracker-printable.md): Discover the top 12 monthly expense tracker printable templates and resources. Find free, editable, and designer-made trackers to manage your budget.
- [The Ultimate List of Household Expenses for 2026: 10 Categories to Track](https://econumo.com/posts/list-of-household-expenses.md): Master your budget with our complete list of household expenses for 2026. This guide covers 10 key categories to help you track spending and save money.
- [How to Reduce Monthly Expenses A Practical Guide for Real Life](https://econumo.com/posts/how-to-reduce-monthly-expenses.md): Learn how to reduce monthly expenses with practical strategies that work. Discover how to audit your spending, cut major bills, and master your budget.
- [How to Stick to a Budget That Actually Works for You](https://econumo.com/posts/how-to-stick-to-a-budget.md): Tired of budgets that fail? Discover a practical method for how to stick to a budget, manage shared finances, and achieve your financial goals with confidence.
- [A Practical Guide to Budgeting for Irregular Income](https://econumo.com/posts/budgeting-for-irregular-income.md): Tired of financial stress? Learn how to manage a variable paycheck with our practical guide to budgeting for irregular income and build lasting stability.
- [How to Budget as a Couple: The Practical Guide](https://econumo.com/posts/how-to-budget-as-a-couple.md): Discover how to budget as a couple with a practical, easy-to-follow plan. Learn to talk money, choose a system, and strengthen your relationship.
- [How to Create a Household Budget That Actually Works for Couples](https://econumo.com/posts/how-to-create-a-household-budget.md): Learn how to create a household budget as a couple. This guide offers practical steps to track spending, set shared goals, and manage your money together.

## Releases

- [v1.0.2](https://econumo.com/releases/v1.0.2.md): v1.0.2 — a maintenance release with dependency updates and a faster, QEMU-free multi-arch release build.
- [v1.0.1](https://econumo.com/releases/v1.0.1.md): v1.0.1 — adds a version and API-docs link to the settings footer, and fixes setting a limit on tags or categories created after a budget.
- [v1.0.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v1.0.0.md): v1.0.0 — a complete rewrite with a new Go backend and redesigned React frontend in a single, much faster and lighter binary.
- [v0.10.1](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.10.1.md): v0.10.1 — adds a version indicator so you always know which build you're running, and fixes a startup issue on Alpine images.
- [v0.10.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.10.0.md): v0.10.0 — raises the character limit for names from 18 to 64 characters and refines the transaction list.
- [v0.9.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.9.0.md): v0.9.0 — Econumo becomes a fully open-source project in a single repository, now under the permissive MIT license.
- [v0.8.1](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.8.1.md): v0.8.1 — enhances Import, letting you override any field during the import process.
- [v0.8.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.8.0.md): v0.8.0 — introduces CSV Import/Export for backing up your data or migrating between Econumo instances.
- [v0.7.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.7.0.md): v0.7.0 — adds experimental, beta-quality PostgreSQL support (not yet recommended for production).
- [v0.6.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.6.0.md): v0.6.0 — rewrites currency handling so each currency can use up to 8 fraction digits for precise budget conversions.
- [v0.5.2](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.5.2.md): v0.5.2 — a small follow-up that fixes an issue with currencies that have no exchange rates.
- [v0.5.1](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.5.1.md): v0.5.1 — bug fixes and UI/UX improvements based on community feedback after the Econumo CE announcement.
- [v0.5.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.5.0.md): v0.5.0 — introduces user onboarding and a transaction calculator widget, plus many budget refinements and fixes.
- [v0.4.3](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.4.3.md): v0.4.3 — adds a running Total to the budgeting module for a clearer big-picture view.
- [v0.4.1](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.4.1.md): v0.4.1 — minor fixes and configuration improvements, including a new ECONUMO_BASE_URL variable.
- [v0.4.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.4.0.md): v0.4.0 — adds user registration along with a broad set of budgeting improvements and bug fixes.
- [v0.3.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.3.0.md): v0.3.0 — Econumo's editions are unified, merging the separate products into the fully open-source Econumo CE.
- [v0.2.3](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.2.3.md): v0.2.3 — small refinements to the login flow, legal pages, and branding.
- [v0.2.2](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.2.2.md): v0.2.2 — a hotfix that restores authentication by rolling back a dependency update that broke it.
- [v0.2.1](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.2.1.md): v0.2.1 — registered users are now connected with one another automatically, making collaboration easier.
- [v0.2.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.2.0.md): v0.2.0 — backend cleanup that drops the legacy PostgreSQL dependency and adds consistent timezone handling.
- [v0.1.0](https://econumo.com/releases/v0.1.0.md): v0.1.0 — the first public release of Econumo, an open, self-hosted way to manage personal and family finances.

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://econumo.com/llms-full.txt): full documentation as a single file
- [RSS](https://econumo.com/posts/index.xml): blog feed
- [Releases RSS](https://econumo.com/releases/index.xml): releases feed
